# EcoSnap – AI for Sustainable Micro Decisions

## 1. Project Overview

EcoSnap is an AI-powered sustainability assistant designed to help users make eco-friendly choices in their daily lives. The system analyzes user habits, context, and available alternatives to provide real-time, personalized sustainability recommendations. The goal is to turn sustainability into simple, consistent daily habits.

---

## 2. Objectives

* Encourage sustainable behavior through small, actionable decisions
* Provide real-time, personalized eco-friendly recommendations
* Use AI to learn user habits and improve suggestions over time
* Promote long-term behavior change without disrupting daily routines

---

## 3. Target Users

* Students and young professionals
* Urban residents
* Environmentally conscious individuals
* Communities aiming to improve sustainability awareness

---

## 4. Functional Requirements

### 4.1 User Management

* User should be able to register and log in
* User should be able to create and manage a basic profile
* System should store user preferences and past actions

### 4.2 Habit Tracking

* System should track daily activities such as shopping, travel, and food choices
* User should be able to provide feedback on recommendations

### 4.3 AI Recommendation Engine

* System should analyze user behavior patterns
* System should generate personalized eco-friendly suggestions
* Recommendations should adapt based on user feedback

### 4.4 Context Awareness

* System should use location and time to improve suggestions
* System should consider availability of local alternatives

### 4.5 Sustainability Scoring

* System should calculate a personal sustainability score
* User should be able to view progress over time

### 4.6 Notifications & Nudges

* System should send real-time sustainability nudges
* Notifications should be non-intrusive and optional

---

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

### 5.1 Performance

* System should respond within acceptable time limits (<2 seconds for suggestions)

### 5.2 Scalability

* System should support increasing number of users
* Architecture should allow future feature expansion

### 5.3 Usability

* Simple and intuitive user interface
* Mobile-first and low-bandwidth friendly design

### 5.4 Security & Privacy

* User data should be securely stored
* No sensitive personal data should be shared without consent

### 5.5 Reliability

* System should function reliably with minimal downtime

---

## 6. Constraints

* Limited access to real-time sustainability datasets
* Dependence on third-party APIs (maps, product data)
* Budget and time limitations for student project

---

## 7. Assumptions

* Users are willing to provide basic habit-related data
* Open or publicly available datasets are sufficient for MVP
* Internet connectivity is available for core features

---

## 8. Success Criteria

* Users receive relevant and timely sustainability suggestions
* Increased user engagement over time
* Positive feedback on ease of use and usefulness

---

## 9. Future Enhancements

* Community-level sustainability insights
* Integration with smart devices
* Multilingual and voice-based interaction
